ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Alert provides a styled static alert composition; shared, deduplicated announcement ownership remains consumer work.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora preserves a concise static Alert by default and can enqueue one explicit polite or assertive summary through the shared announcer without placing visible copy or actions in a live region.

visualSignature.summary: A literal Canvas panel, precise Ink boundary, compact squared severity symbol, strong heading, and restrained semantic signal seam identify the Mergora feedback family.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora preserves a concise static Alert by default and can enqueue one explicit polite or assertive summary through the shared announcer without placing visible copy or actions in a live region.

api.enableWhen: Set live to polite or assertive and provide a non-empty announcement.

disabledBehavior.ui: The visible Alert remains, with no enhancement-only announcement UI.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No shared-announcer effect, queueing, or deduplication behavior runs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No alert/status role, aria-live, aria-atomic, or hidden announcement output is emitted.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated native semantics, naming, keyboard-relevant behavior, axe, narrow layout, RTL, touch, forced-colors, and reduced-motion evidence is current; contract-required manual assistive-technology records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Static rendering, explicit live modes, provider ownership, announcement transitions, deduplication, and clean live-off removal are covered.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Contract-required manual screen-reader, speech, switch, and relevant device evidence is not yet recorded.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Badge provides styled noninteractive labels and variants, without a discriminated localized status/count contract.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora keeps the default category badge lightweight while opt-in status mode localizes severity and count mode visually bounds large values while preserving the exact localized accessible count.

visualSignature.summary: A compact squared label, Ink boundary, readable type, and restrained semantic fills provide status recognition without excessive pill styling.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora keeps the default category badge lightweight while opt-in status mode localizes severity and count mode visually bounds large values while preserving the exact localized accessible count.

api.enableWhen: Set kind to status or count and provide the props required by that discriminated mode.

disabledBehavior.ui: Only the concise category label is rendered; status and count specimens are absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No severity or count formatting path runs.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-only callback or event is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No localized severity prefix, hidden exact count, overflow state, or count label is exposed.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Automated native semantics, naming, keyboard-relevant behavior, axe, narrow layout, RTL, touch, forced-colors, and reduced-motion evidence is current; contract-required manual assistive-technology records remain incomplete.

interactionEvidence.summary: Category, status, count, overflow, localization, wrapping, and complete removal of inactive discriminated-mode output are covered.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn compositions combine Alert and Button for a dismissible banner; controlled persistence, hydration policy, and adapter ownership remain application work.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora Banner supports mutually exclusive controlled and uncontrolled dismissal plus injected persistence with SSR-safe pre-paint resolution and consumer-owned failure handling.

visualSignature.summary: A page-width Canvas surface, strong Ink frame, compact dismiss control, strong heading, and severity seam extend the same feedback-family language to banners.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora Banner supports mutually exclusive controlled and uncontrolled dismissal plus injected persistence with SSR-safe pre-paint resolution and consumer-owned failure handling.

api.enableWhen: Use uncontrolled dismissal and provide a BannerPersistenceAdapter.

disabledBehavior.ui: No persistence-pending suppression state or persistence-specific UI is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No adapter read, write, or pre-paint persistence resolution runs; ordinary dismissal remains available when dismissible.
disabledBehavior.events: No persistence error event can fire.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No persistence-specific accessible output is emitted; the named non-live aside contract remains unchanged.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled and uncontrolled dismissal, persistence read/write timing, SSR hydration, touch target, focus states, and persistence omission are covered.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora Callout stays deliberately non-live and can opt into a correctly named complementary landmark for important explanatory regions without changing its visible composition.

visualSignature.summary: A literal Canvas surface, Ink edge, compact contextual symbol, strong title, and restrained violet/green signal distinguish explanatory content from generic tinted cards.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora Callout stays deliberately non-live and can opt into a correctly named complementary landmark for important explanatory regions without changing its visible composition.

disabledBehavior.ui: The same visible callout remains without enhancement-only UI.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The root stays a neutral div.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-only callback or event is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No complementary landmark or accessible landmark name is exposed.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora EmptyState preserves native consumer-owned actions and can add a validated labelled list of concrete recovery suggestions that disappears completely when omitted.

visualSignature.summary: A literal Canvas, Ink frame, compact violet context marker, strong heading, and edge-to-edge recovery rail create a workbench state rather than a generic centered card.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora EmptyState preserves native consumer-owned actions and can add a validated labelled list of concrete recovery suggestions that disappears completely when omitted.

api.enableWhen: Provide recoverySuggestions with a non-empty label and at least one non-empty item.

disabledBehavior.ui: No suggestion rail, label, or item list is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No suggestion validation or rendering path runs; primary and secondary recovery actions remain unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-only callback or event is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No labelled suggestion list or suggestion items enter the accessibility tree.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora ErrorState has an explicit recoverable retry contract and can reveal technical context through a native disclosure without forcing diagnostics into the concise default state.

visualSignature.summary: A literal Canvas, strong Ink and danger seam, compact error marker, firm heading, and restrained native action/disclosure treatment make recovery clear without decorative clutter.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora ErrorState has an explicit recoverable retry contract and can reveal technical context through a native disclosure without forcing diagnostics into the concise default state.

disabledBehavior.ui: No technical-details summary, panel, or diagnostic rail is rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No disclosure interaction exists; recoverable retry behavior remains independently available.
disabledBehavior.events: No disclosure toggle event or enhancement-only callback is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No details/summary disclosure semantics or technical text enter the accessibility tree.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora preserves native meter semantics and can render localized low, high, and optimum thresholds as visible context linked with aria-describedby.

visualSignature.summary: Ink typography and measurement structure pair with a restrained green native signal and a non-pill threshold rail.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora preserves native meter semantics and can render localized low, high, and optimum thresholds as visible context linked with aria-describedby.

disabledBehavior.ui: No threshold summary rail or threshold labels are rendered.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No threshold-summary formatting work runs; the native meter continues unchanged.
disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-only callback or event is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No generated threshold description or aria-describedby relationship is added.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Progress provides an accessible styled progress bar, commonly without a built-in visible localized value context.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora uses native progress semantics with a visible localized value context that can be independently removed while preserving the visible label and determinate or indeterminate model.

visualSignature.summary: Ink label hierarchy, a crisp native track, Living Green determinate fill, and Deep Violet indeterminate signal form a precise Mergora measurement pattern.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora uses native progress semantics with a visible localized value context that can be independently removed while preserving the visible label and determinate or indeterminate model.

api.enableWhen: Keep showValue true, which is the backward-compatible default.

disabledBehavior.ui: The visible localized value is not rendered.

disabledBehavior.events: No enhancement-only callback or event is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: aria-valuetext is omitted while the visible label continues to name the native progress element.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora Skeleton preserves logical footprint and strict decorative semantics while allowing consumers to remove pulse motion independently without changing geometry.

visualSignature.summary: Restrained corners, a precise Ink-like boundary, Surface fill, and purposeful opacity motion preserve future layout without presenting a decorative card.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora Skeleton preserves logical footprint and strict decorative semantics while allowing consumers to remove pulse motion independently without changing geometry.

api.enableWhen: Keep animated true, which is the backward-compatible default.

disabledBehavior.ui: The same loading geometry remains without animated opacity changes.
disabledBehavior.behavior: The animation data hook and pulse behavior are removed.
disabledBehavior.events: No animation-driven event or enhancement-only callback is produced.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The placeholder remains aria-hidden with no role, label, live-region, or fake-content output.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Ordinary Shadcn Spinner provides decorative loading motion, while ownership of a named busy region and deduplicated announcements remains consumer composition.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora pairs a decorative Spinner with BusyRegion, which owns a named aria-busy relationship and can announce only the start of a busy period through the shared announcer.

visualSignature.summary: A compact Living Green and Deep Violet mark provides recognizable loading state, with a static two-pattern fallback when reduced motion is requested.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Mergora pairs a decorative Spinner with BusyRegion, which owns a named aria-busy relationship and can announce only the start of a busy period through the shared announcer.

api.enableWhen: Render BusyRegion with announce true, busy true, a name, and an optional busyMessage.

disabledBehavior.ui: The decorative Spinner and visible busy content remain without announcement UI.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No announcement effect, queueing, or deduplication behavior runs; the named aria-busy region remains.
disabledBehavior.events: No busy-start announcement event is enqueued.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No live announcement output is emitted; Spinner stays aria-hidden and BusyRegion keeps only its named region and aria-busy semantics.

ordinaryShadcnBaseline.summary: Shadcn supplies Button, Scroll Area and feedback primitives, but consumers must compose read state, grouped/bulk behavior, large lists and a position-safe live-update policy.

mergoraAdvantage.summary: Mergora can queue live notifications outside the active reading position, then reveal them explicitly; grouping, bulk actions, virtualization and announcements remain independent.

visualSignature.summary: A literal white ordered stream uses ink separators, green unread marks, a violet queued-update rail, strong hierarchy, restrained corners and shared focus geometry.

optionalEnhancements.summary: New arrivals wait in a polite contextual rail until explicitly revealed, preserving the current read position.

api.enableWhen: Set liveUpdatePolicy to queue and provide pending count plus reveal callback.

disabledBehavior.ui: The queued-update rail and reveal control are absent.
disabledBehavior.behavior: Pending count is not formatted and reading position is untouched.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No queued-update live region or reveal control enters the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Notifications may be grouped under real category or localized date headings.

api.enableWhen: Set groupBy to category or date.

disabledBehavior.ui: A single flat ordered list remains.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No group map or localized heading is created.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No group headings or nested labelled sections are exposed.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Native checkboxes support explicit visible selection and bulk read-state changes.

disabledBehavior.ui: Selection checkboxes and bulk action rail are absent.
disabledBehavior.events: No bulk read-state callback is emitted.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: No selection controls or bulk action group enters the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: A bounded visible slice and measured spacers support consumer-controlled large-list windowing.

disabledBehavior.ui: Every filtered notification is rendered without spacers.
disabledBehavior.behavior: No slicing or virtual-window validation runs.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The complete filtered sequence remains in the accessibility tree.

optionalEnhancements.summary: Read-state changes can be announced without forcing live output on basic usage.

disabledBehavior.behavior: Read changes do not update announcement text.
disabledBehavior.accessibility: The polite live region is absent.

accessibilityEvidence.summary: Native structure and focused browser/axe fixtures exist; the complete Risk Class 3 manual assistive-technology matrix remains unrecorded.

interactionEvidence.summary: Controlled/uncontrolled read state, exact enhancement isolation, queued reveal, narrow/RTL, forced colors and reduced motion have fixtures; scale and full browsers remain.

parityEvidence.summary: Deterministic generation derives every package implementation byte from normalized canonical source and matches native and Shadcn payloads to the same consumer transform; the recorded exact-tarball Next.js and Vite package/source matrix passes.

blockers.summary: Manual assistive-technology and large-data virtualization/performance evidence is incomplete.

maturityAssessment.rationale: Deterministic delivery parity is verified, but the remaining interaction, lifecycle, browser, accessibility, scale, security, or release evidence recorded for this item is still insufficient for promotion.
